chapter_context.summary: Details King Hezekiah's deep repentance, Isaiah's reassuring prophecy, Hezekiah's passionate prayer, and the swift slaughter of 185,000 Assyrians by the angel of the Lord.

outline.text: King Hezekiah's Repentance and Isaiah's Word of Assurance
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King Hezekiah's Repentance and Isaiah's Word of Assurance (37:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Sennacherib's Blasphemous Letter and Hezekiah's Prayer
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Sennacherib's Blasphemous Letter and Hezekiah's Prayer (37:8-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Isaiah's Prophecy of Assyria's Swift Rejection
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Isaiah's Prophecy of Assyria's Swift Rejection (37:21-35), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Slaughter of 185,000 Assyrians and Death of Sennacherib
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Slaughter of 185,000 Assyrians and Death of Sennacherib (37:36-38), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 37, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
